{"step_by_step_thinking": "The mesentery of the sigmoid colon is a structure that supports and suspends the sigmoid colon in the abdomen, and it is in close proximity to the left ureter. The bifurcation of the common iliac artery is a location where the left ureter passes posteriorly. The quadratus lumborum muscle is located in the lumbar region and is not in direct relation to the left ureter. The gonadal vessels, specifically the ovarian arteries in females, are in close proximity to the left ureter as they cross the pelvic floor. Therefore, the structure that does not lie in relation to the left ureter is the quadratus lumborum.", "answer_choice": "C"}
